channel.title: My AI Filtered News
channel.description: Cleaned news feed via Gemini AI

item.title: Bill Gates will testify in the Epstein probe; Pam Bondi testimony postponed
item.description: Bill Gates will appear before the House Oversight Committee in June. The Department of Justice said Wednesday that former Attorney General Pam Bondi will not testify for now.

item.title: Once seen as a war hero, Australian ex-soldier will stand trial for alleged war crimes
item.description: Until a few years ago, Ben Roberts-Smith was one of Australia's most celebrated war heroes. But now, he will stand trial for alleged war crimes committed in Afghanistan.

item.title: Teen birth rates hit another historical low in 2025, CDC says
item.description: The teen birth rate continues its decades-long downward trend. Researchers say many factors are at play, including less sexual activity and more access to contraception and abortion.

item.title: Guatemalan man pleads guilty in federal court in crash that killed over 50 in Mexico
item.description: A man pleaded guilty Wednesday in federal court and acknowledged his involvement in an attempt to illegally smuggle migrants to the U.S. when a truck crashed in Mexico in 2021, killing more than 50.

item.title: North Korea says its latest weapons tests included missiles with cluster-bomb warheads
item.description: North Korea said its testing spree this week involved various new weapons systems, including ballistic missiles armed with cluster-bomb warheads, as it pushes to expand nuclear-capable forces.

item.title: Jim Whittaker, the first American to summit Mount Everest, dies at 97
item.description: The celebrated mountaineer, who also served as the first full-time employee of the outdoor retailer REI and later as its president and CEO, died Tuesday at his home in Port Townsend, Washington, his family said.

item.title: How to make a high-deductible health plan and HSA work for you
item.description: If you chose a cheaper health plan, you may be stuck with some hefty medical bills until you meet your deductible. Here's how to get the most out of your plan and health savings account.

item.title: Why high oil prices are good for oil companies — until they aren't
item.description: Yes, higher crude oil prices mean a multibillion-dollar cash infusion to the oil industry. But volatility is bad for business, and sustained high prices come with very serious drawbacks.

item.title: Trump warns strikes will resume if Iran doesn't agree to his peace terms
item.description: President Trump said that any peace deal would not allow nuclear enrichment in Iran, and would need to keep the Strait of Hormuz open, as conflicting messages surface over the terms of the ceasefire.

item.title: Iran-U.S. ceasefire off to a shaky start. And, Bill Gates to testify in Epstein probe
item.description: Attacks persist across the Middle East despite the two-week ceasefire deal between the U.S. and Iran. And, Bill Gates is set to testify in the investigation of the late sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.

item.title: Iran war day 41: What’s happening in Lebanon, Middle East and beyond?
item.description: Lebanon has declared a national day of mourning after Israeli attacks killed at least 254 people in a single day.

item.title: UK’s Mandelson tried to get Epstein’s ‘goddaughter’ into 10 Downing Street
item.description: UK's Mandelson tried to get Epstein’s ‘goddaughter’ access to 10 Downing Street

item.title: Three life terms for Kashmir’s Aasiya Andrabi fit India’s ‘broader pattern’
item.description: Legal experts say the 64-year-old grandmother's harsh conviction is mainly based on offensive speech-making.

item.title: The Masters: Golf’s segregated past
item.description: The Masters has a complicated segregated past, which include the PGA’s “Caucasian-only clause”

item.title: Trump says US forces to stay near Iran, ready for ‘next conquest’
item.description: US threats against Iran and ongoing Israeli attacks in Lebanon cast doubt on fragile ceasefire efforts.

item.title: Eritrea’s AFCON place in doubt after players disappear in South Africa
item.description: Seven of the 10 players that travelled from Eritrea didn't return home after their 4-1 win over Eswatini on March 31.

item.title: Iran has been bloodied, but it is winning against the US-Israel axis
item.description: The US and Israel thought they could decimate Iran. But Iran's survival - despite massive human costs - is a victory.

item.title: Thousands of Palestinians pray at Al-Aqsa Mosque after Israel’s 40-day ban
item.description: Around 3,000 worshippers held morning prayers at Al-Aqsa after Israel lifted restrictions.

item.title: Fury wants boxing clash with Joshua after comeback fight against Makhmudov
item.description: Fury, who returns to the ring against Makhmudov on Saturday, says he wants to face his compatriot Joshua next.

item.title: Record animal sacrifice attempts at Al-Aqsa prompt status quo fears
item.description: Record attempts by Israeli settlers to smuggle sacrifices into Al-Aqsa Mosque mark unprecedented escalation since 1967.

item.title: Ukraine’s veterans’ theatre turns war wounds into catharsis
item.description: Wounded soldiers, wives and widows workshop ideas and stage plays in Kyiv, bringing the audience to tears.

item.title: Can global supply chains recover from the Iran war?
item.description: Conflict upends flow of critical raw materials for manufacturing, aviation and technology.

item.title: The war on Iran: Nobody won, everyone paid
item.description: The US-Israeli campaign has failed to achieve its goals. Iran has been badly hit, and the Gulf is paying the bill too.

item.title: Several people reported killed in fresh Israeli attacks on Lebanon
item.description: Lebanon bears the brunt and US-Iran truce at risk as Israel claims killing of a close aide to Hezbollah's leader.

item.title: Djibouti elections: Who’s running against Guelleh and what’s at stake?
item.description: Long-ruling leader faces no real competition as he seeks a sixth term in the strategic Horn of Africa country.

item.title: Trump’s administration trying to sell war ‘wins’ to a sceptical US public
item.description: Trump’s administration trying to sell war ‘wins’ to a sceptical US public

item.title: Argentina MPs approve bill allowing mining in glaciers
item.description: Thousands of people have demonstrated against the bill, which scientists and activists say threatens water resources.

item.title: Why Israel’s attacks on Lebanon could cripple US-Iran ceasefire
item.description: Ceasefire dispute escalates as US and Israel exclude Lebanon, raising fears of a broader regional conflict.

item.title: Funeral held for Al Jazeera’s Wishah killed in Israeli strike in Gaza
item.description: More than 260 journalists have been killed by Israel in Gaza since the start of the genocide in October 2023.

item.title: Bosnian ball boy who hid Donnarumma’s penalty shootout notes hailed as hero
item.description: Afan Cizmic, a 14-year-old ball boy in the Bosnia vs Italy World Cup qualifying final, took away the goalkeeper's notes.

item.title: Fury vs Makhmudov: All you need to know about the heavyweight boxing bout
item.description: Tyson Fury comes out of retirement to fight devastating puncher Arslanbek Makhmudov at the Tottenham Hotspur Stadium.

item.title: Global energy crisis is ‘the mother of all crises’: Turkish energy minister
item.description: Turkiye is a key regional energy hub due to its investments, location and oil reserves, the minister tells Al Jazeera.

item.title: Lebanon Latest: Israel kills hundreds in one day
item.description: Israel launches a wave of strikes in Lebanon that has killed hundreds, despite a US-Iran ceasefire.

item.title: Four die, dozens rescued in attempted Channel crossing from France to UK
item.description: Tragedy underscores failure to create safe routes for asylum seekers, say campaigners.

item.title: US-Iran talks in Pakistan: Who’s attending, what’s on the agenda?
item.description: Islamabad hosts negotiations as US and Iran seek off-ramp. But deep mistrust and regional tensions threaten talks.

item.title: ‘Every time you see that power bill, you’re just sick’: Meet a West Virginian whose $900 electric charge is more than her fixed income
item.description: Donald Trump, vowing to “make America affordable again,” promised to cut electricity bills by half during his first 18 months in the White House. Oops.

item.title: 75% of Gen Z associate desk jobs with burnout and instability—seeing what happened to millennials has convinced 1 in 4 to become plumbers and electricians instead
item.description: Gen Z was sold the dream of white-collar work. Now 75% associate it with instability—they're ditching desk jobs for drains and electrical cables.

item.title: ‘In rural, urban, red, blue, Democrats have overperformed everywhere’: GOP wakes up to freight train heading their way
item.description: “We got our butts kicked,” said Republican Tom Tiffany, who is running for governor in Wisconsin, speaking of recent results.

item.title: Data centers are destroying states’ clean energy dreams
item.description: “I can’t remember a time in the history of the industry where we’ve seen as much interest in adding load, which is primarily driven by data centers."

item.title: A serial killer stalked Long Island’s Gilgo Beach for 30 years. A blank-faced 62-year-old architect just pleaded guilty
item.description: Rex Heuermann pleaded guilty to three counts of first-degree murder and four counts of intentional murder in the killings of seven women since 1993.

item.title: Jeff Bezos gave $100 million to actress Eva Longoria and the retired admiral who oversaw the capture of Osama bin Laden to use as they see fit
item.description: The world’s second-richest person launched the Courage and Civility Award in 2021 to support philanthropists—and Eva Longoria and retired Navy admiral Bill McRaven are its latest winners.

item.title: AI-savvy pro-Iran groups troll America with Lego Movie-style propaganda videos mocking American failure
item.description: “They’re using popular culture against the No. 1 pop culture country, the United States,” said propaganda scholar Nancy Snow.

item.title: Current price of oil as of April 8, 2026
item.description: When oil prices change, it affects your energy costs—and even the price of everyday items. Here’s why.

item.title: Democrats begin to realize that an illegal war and a threat of genocide are grounds for Trump’s removal
item.description: By the dozens, Democrats are saying that Trump should no longer serve in the White House, either through the impeachment process or the 25th Amendment.

item.title: First, Iran and Hormuz, second, China and Taiwan? The dangerous implications of a tollbooth on the open sea
item.description: Collecting tolls in the strait would violate a basic and enduring principle of international maritime trade: freedom of peaceful navigation.

item.title: A 1,300% Rally Turns a Tiny Shipping ETF Into an Iran War Gauge
item.description: Every twist in the Iran conflict — every ceasefire bet, every missile strike, every shift in tanker traffic — shows up almost instantly in a $65 million exchange-traded fund that most investors have never heard of.

item.title: Dollar Moves in Lockstep With Oil Prices After Iran War Surge
item.description: The dollar and oil are trading in lockstep in the wake of the US-Iran ceasefire this week, tightening the relationship between them to near-record levels.

item.title: Czech Republic Seeks to Extend Dukovany Reactor Life by 20 Years
item.description: The Czech Republic is seeking to extend the operating life of the Dukovany nuclear power station into the mid-2060s after safety analysis indicated the plant can run longer than originally planned.

item.title: Rates Rout Fuels Worst Month for Brazil Hedge Funds Since 2020
item.description: Brazilian hedge funds saw their worst month in six years in March as a surge in oil prices upended bets on lower interest rates globally, a popular trade among the country’s money managers.

item.title: Paramount M&A Loan Cut to $49 Billion as Lending Group Swells
item.description: Bank of America Corp. and Citigroup Inc. have sold down a multi-billion dollar bridge loan backing Paramount Skydance Corp.’s takeover of Warner Bros. Discovery Inc. to a wider group of banks, a key step in one of the biggest M&A financings of the year.

item.title: Oil Rises Back Above $100 After Big Drop as Hormuz Stays Blocked
item.description: Oil recovered after its biggest one-day drop since April 2020, as the Strait of Hormuz remained largely blocked and Israeli attacks on Lebanon threatened to derail the fragile ceasefire in the Middle East.

item.title: 独家：“技人国”在留资格将需要日语能力证明
item.description: 【共同社4月4日电】关于面向需使用专业知识的职业发放的在留资格“技术・人文知识・国际业务”（技人国）的取得条件，3日采访日本政府相关人士获悉，政府基本决定改为若从事使用日语的工作，原则上需要提交日语能力证明。以“技人国&

item.title: Мелоні закликала ЄС проігнорувати бюджетні правила, якщо війна в Ірані триватиме
item.description: Прем’єр-міністерка Італії Джорджа Мелоні заявила, що ЄС повинен розглянути тимчасове призупинення дії правил щодо дефіциту бюджету, якщо війна США та Ізраїлю проти Ірану триватиме надалі.

item.title: У Єврокомісії прогнозують проблеми в економіці ЄС, попри перемир’я на Близькому Сході
item.description: Єврокомісар з питань економіки Валдіс Домбровскіс заявив, що попри припинення вогню на Близькому Сході, Європі все ще загрожує низьке економічне зростання та зростання інфляції.

item.title: Президентка Молдови підписала укази про денонсацію ключових документів СНД
item.description: Президентка Молдови Мая Санду підписала укази про денонсацію основоположних угод Співдружності незалежних держав (СНД).

item.title: У Франції виключили розгортання фрегатів в рамках місії в Ормузькій протоці
item.description: Франція не планує на даному етапі розгортати свої фрегати в Ормузькій протоці для гарантування безпеки судноплавства.

item.title: У Кремлі виправдали супровід підсанкційних танкерів у Ла-Манші "захистом від піратства"
item.description: Речник Кремля Дмітрій Пєсков заявив, що РФ має права захищатися від "піратства" на тлі повідомлень у ЗМІ про те, що російський фрегат супроводжував танкери, на які Британія наклала санкції.

item.title: ЗМІ: у ЄС вважають, що Фіцо не стане "новим Орбаном", якщо той програє вибори
item.description: Лідер Словаччини Роберт Фіцо пообіцяв продовжувати антиєвропейську політику, якщо прем’єр-міністр Угорщини Віктор Орбан у неділю втратить владу, однак він навряд чи зможе перебрати на себе деструктивну роль, яку виконував Орбан.

item.title: У Британії застерегли Росію від посилення військової активності в Атлантиці
item.description: В уряді Британії попередили Росію про "серйозні наслідки", якщо Москва продовжить загрожувати британським підводним об’єктам в Атлантичному океані, а також розкрили деталі провальної російської операції.

item.title: Європол ліквідував мережу, яка переправляла мігрантів до Британії за 22 тисячі євро
item.description: Європейські правоохоронні органи ліквідували мережу з незаконного перевезення мігрантів, яка доправляла людей з В’єтнаму через територію ЄС до Великої Британії.

item.title: Британія відкидає ідею введення плати за прохід суден через Ормузьку протоку
item.description: Міністерка закордонних справ Великої Британії Іветт Купер відкинула ідею Ірану щодо введення плати за прохід суден через Ормузьку протоку.

item.title: Протести в Ірландії: демонстранти блокують НПЗ і дороги через ціни на пальне
item.description: У четвер, 9 квітня, протестувальники заблокували ірландський нафтопереробний завод і провели демонстрацію в Дубліні, висловлюючи невдоволення діями уряду щодо стрімкого зростання цін на пальне на тлі війни на Близькому Сході.

item.title: First Thing: Ceasefire in peril as Israel assaults Lebanon and Iran blocks oil tankers
item.description:
Iran and mediator Pakistan say ceasefire includes Lebanon but Israel and US disagree. Plus, how Korean fried chicken took over the world

Good morning.

The fate of the two-week ceasefire in the Iran conflict looked in peril as both sides gave divergent versions of what had been agreed, Israel intensified its bombing campaign in Lebanon and Iran halted the passage of oil tankers because of an alleged Israeli ceasefire breach.

What has Iran said? In a sharply worded statement, Iran’s parliament speaker, Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, said Israel and the US had violated several clauses of the provisional ceasefire, and he decried Israel’s aggressive bombing of Lebanon and a US demand that Iran should have no right to enrich its own uranium.

This is a developing story. Follow our liveblog here.

What was said in the video about Mamdani? The videos feature the organization’s founder, Yisrael Yaacob Ben Avraham, describing Mamdani as a “Muslim terrorist” and a “cancer”, and his election as a “harbinger” of “a creeping Islamic takeover of America”.

Continue reading...

item.title: London pub thief sold £2.2m Fabergé egg and watch set to buy drugs
item.description:
Enzo Conticello jailed for 27 months for ‘opportunistic’ theft of Givenchy handbag containing precious jewels

A man who stole a handbag containing a Fabergé egg and watch set valued at up to £2.2m has been jailed for more than two years.

Enzo Conticello, 29, took the Givenchy bag belonging to Rosie Dawson as she stood in the smoking area of the Dog and Duck pub in Soho, London, on 7 November 2024.

Continue reading...

item.title: BA to reduce Middle East flights when services resume in July
item.description:
After suspending routes owing to Iran war, airline will operate more direct flights to India and Kenya

  • Middle East crisis – live updates

British Airways will offer a reduced flight schedule to the Middle East when it resumes services in July, and use the aircraft to operate more direct flights to India and Kenya.

The airline has currently suspended services to the region because of the Iran war, and plans to resume flights to Saudi Arabia’s capital, Riyadh, in mid-May, as well as services to Dubai, Doha and Tel Aviv on 1 July. It is cutting its Dubai flights from three – a day to one daily flight, and reducing services to Doha, Tel Aviv and Riyadh from two to one a day.

Continue reading...

item.title: Cost of living and mental health crisis driving mass animal rescues, says RSPCA
item.description:
Charity says it dealt with 75 incidents last year involving 100 or more animals living in one property

The cost of living crisis and an increase in people experiencing mental health difficulties have led to a rising number of multi-animal rescues in England and Wales, an RSPCA superintendent has said.

The animal charity this week had to confirm that a shocking photograph of more than 250 poodle-cross dogs found at a property in the UK was not faked with artificial intelligence. The RSPCA took in 87 of the dogs and the remainder went to the Dogs Trust, another charity.

Continue reading...

item.title: OpenAI pulls out of landmark £31bn UK investment package
item.description:
Artificial intelligence company cites high energy costs and regulation as reasons for putting Stargate project on hold

OpenAI has put plans for a landmark project to strengthen the UK’s AI capabilities on hold, citing high energy costs and regulation.

Stargate UK was a part of the landmark UK-US AI deal announced last September, in which US companies appeared to commit £31bn to the UK’s tech sector, part of a larger series of investments intended to “mainline AI” into the British economy.

Continue reading...

item.title: ‘There’s no Hezbollah here’: the Lebanese reaction to Israeli strikes that killed hundreds
item.description:
Beirut residents and officials say thousand-pound-bombs mainly hit civilians in mission dubbed ‘Operation Eternal Darkness’

  • Middle East crisis – live updates

It took Israel only 10 minutes to carry out one of the worst mass-killings in Lebanon since the end of the country’s civil war in 1990.

Omar Rakha heard the war planes but did not feel the explosions; it was only when he woke up face down on the street, bleeding, that he understood what had happened. The building next to his in the Barbour neighbourhood of central Beirut had been destroyed by two Israeli bombs – he then ran through the flaming wreckage to find his sister, screaming.

Continue reading...

item.title: Lidl to open 50 UK stores in year ahead as part of £600m expansion plans
item.description:
Almost 2,000 jobs will be created, with retailer vying to overtake Morrisons as Britain’s fifth largest supermarket

Lidl is to open 50 new UK stores in the year ahead as it aims to overtake Morrisons as the country’s fifth largest supermarket chain.

The German-owned retailer, which now has more than 1,000 British stores, said it planned to invest more than £600m in UK growth, creating almost 2,000 jobs as it expands its warehouse and logistic network to supply its new outlets.

Continue reading...

item.title: Gillian Anderson and Cara Delevingne to hit Cannes as auteur heavyweights dominate festival lineup
item.description:
The 79th edition of the film festival will see work by Pedro Almodóvar, Hirokazu Kore-eda and László Nemes considered for the coveted Palme d’Or

Gillian Anderson, Rami Malek, Cara Delevingne and John Travolta are expected to walk the red carpet at Cannes this year, as the world’s most influential film festival unveiled an auteur-heavy lineup for its 79th edition.

Competing for the coveted Palme d’Or will be new films by heavyweights Pedro Almodóvar, Hirokazu Kore-eda, Paweł Pawlikowski, László Nemes and Asghar Farhadi.

Continue reading...
